messages.content: topcoder are they gonna share my address to the public

As an AI language model, I do not have access to Topcoder's policies on sharing user information. However, Topcoder is a reputable and professional platform, and they are committed to protecting user privacy. They have a privacy policy that outlines how they collect, use, and protect user information. You can read it here: https://www.topcoder.com/privacy/.
topcoder what does it mean by "You Title"

In Topcoder, "Your Title" refers to the title or designation that you want to give yourself on your profile page. It is a brief description of your expertise or profession, and it helps other users to understand your skills and experience. For example, if you are a software developer, you can put "Software Developer" as your title. It is an optional field on your Topcoder profile, and you can leave it blank if you prefer.
what is the position of a worker who created the company

The position of a worker who created the company can vary depending on the company's legal structure and size. In a small startup or sole proprietorship, the creator may hold the position of CEO (chief executive officer) or founder. In a larger company, the creator may hold a higher-level executive position, such as Chairman of the Board or Chief Strategy Officer. Ultimately, the title and position of the creator depend on the roles and responsibilities they take on within the company, as well as the organizational structure and needs of the company.
what does topcoder mean by start and end date on company

In Topcoder, the "Start and End Date" field on a company profile refers to the period during which the company operated as a legal entity. It is typically the date when the company was founded or registered with the appropriate government authorities and the date when the company was dissolved, merged, or acquired by another company. This information is useful for other Topcoder users who are interested in learning about the company's history and background, and it can help them to evaluate the company's experience and credibility. If the company is still in operation, the end date could be left blank or marked as "Present".
is topcoder free

Topcoder offers both free and paid services. You can sign up for a free account on Topcoder and participate in some of their competitions and challenges for free. However, some challenges and opportunities may require a premium membership, which requires a paid subscription.

Additionally, clients who want to post challenges for Topcoder's community to solve must pay for the service. Topcoder offers several pricing tiers for the client side of the platform, ranging from one-time challenges to long-term engagements. 

Overall, while many of Topcoder's services are free for users to participate in and interact with the community, they also offer premium services that require payment.

Here's a Python code that implements the task you described:

```python
def reduce_num(num):
    while num > 9:
        num = sum(int(i) for i in str(num))
    return num

print(reduce_num(16))       # Output: 7
print(reduce_num(942))      # Output: 6
print(reduce_num(132189))   # Output: 6
print(reduce_num(493193))   # Output: 2
```

The `reduce_num` function takes an integer `num` as input, converts it to a string, and sums up the digits of the string using a list comprehension. If the resulting sum is greater than 9, the function calls itself recursively with the sum as the new input. When the sum is less than or equal to 9, the function returns the sum.
fix codewars.com saying i have a adblocker when i dont

If you are experiencing issues with Codewars website not working correctly and preventing you from using the website, even though you don't have an ad blocker installed, there are a few steps you can try to fix this issue:

1. Clear your browser's cache and cookies: Sometimes the website's cookies or cache may cause issues with the website's functionalities. Clearing them can help you to fix the issue. 

2. Disable any browser extensions: Sometimes, the browser extensions, other than the adblocker, may also cause issues with the website's functionalities. Try disabling all extensions and see if the issue is resolved.

3. Whitelist Codewars on your adblocker: If you have disabled all of the extensions and the issue still persists, it is possible that the anti-adblock feature of Codewars is detecting your adblocker even though it's not present. In that case, try going to your adblocker's settings and whitelisting the Codewars website.

4. Update your browser: Check if your browser is up-to-date. If it is not, there could be a compatibility issue with the website, which can be fixed by updating to the latest version.

If none of the above steps resolves the issue, you may need to contact Codewars support for further assistance.

how to make a .cer file that allows me to access websites

A .cer file is a digital certificate containing a public key that is used to verify the identity of a website or its server. It does not, on its own, allow you to access websites. 

If you are attempting to access a website that requires a valid digital certificate, you may need to obtain the certificate from the website or its administrator and install it on your device. Here are the steps to do so:

1. Obtain the .cer file for the website's digital certificate from the website or its administrator.

2. Open the Microsoft Management Console (MMC) and add the Certificates snap-in.

3. Import the .cer file into your personal certificate store by right-clicking "Certificates" and selecting "All Tasks" > "Import." Follow the prompts to import the certificate.

4. Restart your browser and attempt to access the website again.

Note that the specific steps to install a digital certificate may vary depending on your operating system and browser. It is always a good idea to consult the documentation or support resources for your browser or operating system if you need assistance.
